data.description:
## Why
Code-review advisory volume is ~5 findings/review, dominated by docs + llm-prompts nits (0/9 high-value
in adjudication), while the highest-value landmine - new behavior shipped with no test - is buried by
the old scalar (0.415). We want less nit friction and a reliable catch for the landmine class.

## What
- Down-rank/suppress the `docs` and `llm-prompts` advisory surfaces and the "documentation-ahead-of-a-
  landed-sibling" pattern that fired repeatedly as false-major nits.
- Add a DET-style "new-behavior-without-test" detector: a new exported/public symbol or new branch in
  the diff with no accompanying test-file change. Ship it advisory/permissive first (the most objective
  landmine class; it sidesteps the diluted scalar).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/criteria_routing.json` (down-rank docs/llm-prompts surfacing), a new
  detector in `detectors.py`, and coach surfacing. No LLM-priority-threshold blocking here (that depends
  on the code-review impact redesign child).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs` + `llm-prompts` nit surfaces are down-ranked/suppressed; nit-volume drop measured on the
      sampled diffs used in the analysis.
- [ ] A deterministic "new-behavior-without-test" detector exists (new public symbol/branch with no test
      change), advisory/permissive, fail-open on ambiguity.
- [ ] Regression eval cases: must-fire on a real untested-new-behavior landmine; must-not-fire on a
      change that adds/updates a covering test.
data.title: Code-review near-term nit reduction + new-behavior-without-test landmine detector
